description: Improve deploy.sh: remove redundant duplicate health check in post-deploy diagnostics section. The script performs a proper health check with error handling (lines 86-92), then immediately does another health check in the post-deploy diagnostics block (line 110) that serves no purpose since the script already validated health. Also source panel.env before running deploy-report.sh so it has proper variables available, instead of the fragile background subshell approach.
steps: In deploy.sh, clean up the post-deploy diagnostics section (lines 97-118). Remove the redundant health check curl (it was already checked with proper error handling above). Keep the journal logs and diagnostics output which are useful for debugging.

Replace the last line's fragile '(source /opt/koris-next/panel.env 2>/dev/null; bash ./deploy-report.sh) 2>/dev/null &' with a cleaner approach: source panel.env if it exists and then call deploy-report.sh in background without suppressing all stderr.
Add a comment clarifying that deploy-report.sh is optional (sends logs to GitHub Issues for remote debugging) and its failure should not affect deploy success.
acceptance_criteria: deploy.sh has exactly one health check with error handling (the existing one at lines 86-92)
The post-deploy diagnostics section does not repeat the health check
deploy-report.sh invocation is cleaner and sources panel.env properly
bash -n deploy.sh passes without syntax errors
verification: Run: grep -c 'curl.*api/health' deploy.sh (should be 1, the single proper health check)
findings: Removed redundant health check curl from post-deploy diagnostics (was a duplicate of the proper health check above). Replaced fragile deploy-report.sh invocation that sourced from /opt/koris-next/panel.env (non-standard path) and suppressed all stderr, with a cleaner approach that sources /etc/panel/panel.env (the canonical path used elsewhere in the script) and runs deploy-report.sh in background without suppressing stderr. Post-review fix: restored subshell isolation for source+exec (`( source ... 2>/dev/null; exec bash ./deploy-report.sh ) &`) and made deploy-report.sh honor existing PANEL_ADDR env var via ${PANEL_ADDR:-$(grep ...)} pattern.
